title: cleome angustifolia subsp. diandra
intro: Cleome angustifolia subsp. diandra, also known as the Narrow-leaved Spider Flower, is a perennial plant native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. It is a member of the Cleomaceae family and has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The plant's roots, leaves, and flowers have been used to treat various health issues, such as fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its health benefits, including its antiseptic and expectorant properties. In herbal preparations, Cleome angustifolia subsp. diandra can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal properties and provide relief from various ailments.
